successCriteria.description: src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts exists and exports Employee, CreateEmployeeDto, UpdateEmployeeDto, and EmployeeFilters.
src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts exists and exports IEmployeeRepository interface and PgEmployeeRepository class.
src/modules/policy/policy.model.ts exists and exports LeavePolicy, CreateLeavePolicyDto, UpdateLeavePolicyDto, and PolicyFilters.
src/modules/policy/policy.repository.ts exists and exports ILeavePolicyRepository interface and PgLeavePolicyRepository class.
Jest unit tests in tests/unit/modules/employee/ cover all IEmployeeRepository methods with >80% line coverage and pass.
Jest unit tests in tests/unit/modules/policy/ cover all ILeavePolicyRepository methods with >80% line coverage and pass.
All repository methods use parameterized queries (no string concatenation) to prevent SQL injection.
Repository implementations do not log employee PII (names, emails) or policy details.
All async repository methods catch and propagate errors as DatabaseError instances; no unhandled promise rejections.
Repository methods accept an optional PoolClient to support transaction coordination, enabling atomic audit writes in future phases (GP-002).

constraints: All database access must go through repository interfaces (Repository pattern).
Repository methods must use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.
Repository implementations must not log employee PII (names, emails) or policy details.
All async repository methods must catch and propagate errors as DatabaseError instances; no unhandled promise rejections.
Repository methods must accept an optional PoolClient to support transaction coordination for future audit writes.
outOfScope: Input validation at API boundaries (deferred to service/controller layers)
